info.title: FHIR Server
info.description:
A FHIR R4-compliant REST API server for managing healthcare resources.

This server provides CRUD operations for core FHIR resources including Patient,
Practitioner, and Encounter. All resources are validated against the HL7 FHIR R4
specification using the `fhir.resources` library.

Designed for integration with AI agents via FastMCP dynamic tool generation.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all FHIR Patient resources stored in the system.

Returns an array of complete Patient resources. Each resource includes all demographics,
identifiers, contact information, and addresses. Requires `patient:read` permission.

Returns an empty array if no patients exist.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of patients.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

post.summary: Create a new FHIR Patient resource
post.description:
Create a new FHIR Patient resource in the system.

This endpoint accepts a structured Patient payload conforming to the HL7 FHIR R4 specification,
validates it against the FHIR Patient schema, and persists it to the database. The server
auto-generates a unique internal ID for the resource.

The payload must include a `resourceType` of "Patient". Recommended fields include at least
one `name` with `use: "official"`, a `gender`, and a `birthDate`. Additional fields such as
`identifier`, `telecom`, and `address` provide richer demographic data.

Returns the complete FHIR Patient resource including the server-assigned `id`.

201.description: Patient successfully created and persisted to the database.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Patient payload — the request body failed FHIR schema validation.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

get.summary: Retrieve a Patient resource by ID
get.description:
Retrieve a single FHIR Patient resource by its internal database ID.

Returns the full Patient resource including all demographics, identifiers, contact
information, and addresses. Returns 404 if no patient exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Patient ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource. Must be a positive integer.

200.description: Patient resource found and returned successfully.

404.description: No Patient resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

put.summary: Update an existing Patient resource (full replacement)
put.description:
Update an existing FHIR Patient resource by replacing it entirely with the provided payload.

This implements FHIR-standard PUT semantics — the entire resource is replaced, not merged.
Any fields not included in the request body will be removed from the resource. To preserve
existing data, include all current fields in the request.

The `id` path parameter identifies the patient to update. The request body must be a valid
FHIR Patient resource. Returns 404 if no patient exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Patient ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Patient payload — failed FHIR schema validation.

404.description: No Patient resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

delete.summary: Delete a Patient resource by ID
delete.description:
Permanently delete a FHIR Patient resource by its internal database ID.

This operation is irreversible. All associated data (identifiers, names, telecoms,
addresses) will also be deleted via cascade. Returns 404 if no patient exists with the given ID.

Returns no content on successful deletion (HTTP 204).

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Patient ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Patient resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

404.description: No Patient resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all FHIR Practitioner resources stored in the system.

Returns an array of complete Practitioner resources. Each resource includes all demographics,
identifiers, contact information, addresses, and professional qualifications.

Returns an empty array if no practitioners exist.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of practitioners.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

post.summary: Create a new FHIR Practitioner resource
post.description:
Create a new FHIR Practitioner resource in the system.

This endpoint accepts a structured Practitioner payload conforming to the HL7 FHIR R4
specification, validates it against the FHIR Practitioner schema, and persists it to the
database. The server auto-generates a unique internal ID for the resource.

A Practitioner represents a person who is directly or indirectly involved in the
provisioning of healthcare — including physicians, nurses, pharmacists, and administrative staff.

Recommended fields include at least one `name` with professional prefix (e.g., "Dr."),
`gender`, `telecom` (work phone/email), and `qualification` entries.

Returns the complete FHIR Practitioner resource including the server-assigned `id`.

201.description: Practitioner successfully created and persisted to the database.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Practitioner payload — failed FHIR schema validation.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

get.summary: Retrieve a Practitioner resource by ID
get.description:
Retrieve a single FHIR Practitioner resource by its internal database ID.

Returns the full Practitioner resource including all demographics, identifiers, contact
information, addresses, and professional qualifications. Returns 404 if no practitioner
exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Practitioner ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource. Must be a positive integer.

200.description: Practitioner resource found and returned successfully.

404.description: No Practitioner resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

put.summary: Update an existing Practitioner resource (full replacement)
put.description:
Update an existing FHIR Practitioner resource by replacing it entirely with the provided payload.

This implements FHIR-standard PUT semantics — the entire resource is replaced, not merged.
Any fields not included in the request body will be removed from the resource. To preserve
existing data, include all current fields in the request.

The `id` path parameter identifies the practitioner to update. Returns 404 if no
practitioner exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Practitioner ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Practitioner payload — failed FHIR schema validation.

404.description: No Practitioner resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

delete.summary: Delete a Practitioner resource by ID
delete.description:
Permanently delete a FHIR Practitioner resource by its internal database ID.

This operation is irreversible. All associated data (identifiers, names, telecoms,
addresses, qualifications) will also be deleted via cascade. Returns 404 if no
practitioner exists with the given ID.

Returns no content on successful deletion (HTTP 204).

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Practitioner ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Practitioner resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

404.description: No Practitioner resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all FHIR Encounter resources stored in the system.

Returns an array of complete Encounter resources. Each resource includes status,
classification, subject, participants, diagnoses, locations, and time period.

Returns an empty array if no encounters exist.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of encounters.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

post.summary: Create a new FHIR Encounter resource
post.description:
Create a new FHIR Encounter resource representing a clinical interaction.

An Encounter documents a patient's visit to a healthcare facility — including clinic visits,
hospital admissions, emergency department visits, and telehealth sessions. The server
validates the payload against the FHIR Encounter schema before persisting.

Required fields: `status` (the encounter lifecycle state) and `class` (the care setting
classification such as "inpatient", "outpatient", or "emergency").

Recommended: Include a `subject` reference to the Patient (e.g., "Patient/123"),
at least one `participant` with a Practitioner reference, and a `period` with start/end times.

Returns the complete FHIR Encounter resource including the server-assigned `id`.

201.description: Encounter successfully created and persisted to the database.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Encounter payload — failed FHIR schema validation.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

get.summary: Retrieve an Encounter resource by ID
get.description:
Retrieve a single FHIR Encounter resource by its internal database ID.

Returns the full Encounter resource including status, classification, subject reference,
participants, diagnoses, locations, reason codes, and time period. Returns 404 if no
encounter exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Encounter ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource. Must be a positive integer.

200.description: Encounter resource found and returned successfully.

404.description: No Encounter resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

put.summary: Update an existing Encounter resource (full replacement)
put.description:
Update an existing FHIR Encounter resource by replacing it entirely with the provided payload.

This implements FHIR-standard PUT semantics — the entire resource is replaced, not merged.
Any fields not included in the request body will be removed from the resource. To preserve
existing data, include all current fields in the request.

Required fields: `status` and `class`. The `id` path parameter identifies the encounter
to update. Returns 404 if no encounter exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Encounter ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource to update. Must be a positive integer.

400.description: Invalid FHIR Encounter payload — failed FHIR schema validation.

404.description: No Encounter resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

422.description: Validation error — the request body is malformed or missing required fields.

delete.summary: Delete an Encounter resource by ID
delete.description:
Permanently delete a FHIR Encounter resource by its internal database ID.

This operation is irreversible. All associated data (types, participants, diagnoses,
locations, reason codes) will also be deleted via cascade. Returns 404 if no encounter
exists with the given ID.

Returns no content on successful deletion (HTTP 204).

parameters.name: id

schema.title: Encounter ID
schema.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

parameters.description: The internal database identifier of the Encounter resource to delete. Must be a positive integer.

404.description: No Encounter resource exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Authentication required — missing or invalid authorization credentials.

coding.title: Codings
coding.description: A list of codes from terminology systems that represent this concept. Multiple codings can represent the same concept in different code systems.

text.title: Text Description
text.description: A human-readable plain text representation of the concept, used as a fallback when no coding is available.

CodeableConcept.title: CodeableConcept
CodeableConcept.description: A concept that may be defined by one or more coding systems, plus optional free text.

system.title: Code System URI
system.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the code. Common systems include 'http://snomed.info/sct' (SNOMED CT) and 'http://loinc.org' (LOINC).

code.title: Code Value
code.description: The code from the code system that represents the concept.

display.title: Display Text
display.description: A human-readable representation of the code for display purposes.

Coding.title: Coding
Coding.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system (e.g., SNOMED CT, ICD-10, LOINC).

code.title: Class Code
code.description: The classification code for the encounter. Common values: 'inpatient' (hospital admission), 'outpatient' (clinic visit), 'emergency' (ER visit), 'virtual' (telehealth).

EncounterClass.title: EncounterClass
EncounterClass.description: The classification of the encounter — determines the care setting.

resourceType.title: Resource Type
resourceType.description: The FHIR resource type. Must always be 'Encounter' for this schema.

status.title: Encounter Status
status.description: The current state of the encounter lifecycle. 'planned' for scheduled visits, 'in-progress' for active encounters, 'finished' for completed visits, 'cancelled' for cancelled appointments, 'entered-in-error' for mistaken entries.

class.title: Encounter Classification
class.description: The classification of the encounter which determines the care setting. This field uses the FHIR alias 'class'. Provide as {'code': 'outpatient'} or similar.

type.title: Encounter Type
type.description: The specific type of encounter — e.g. 'Consultation', 'Follow-up', 'Annual physical'. Use SNOMED CT codes when available.

subject.title: Patient Reference
subject.description: A FHIR reference to the Patient who is the subject of this encounter, in the format 'Patient/ID'. This is the patient being seen.

participant.title: Encounter Participants
participant.description: Healthcare providers who participated in the encounter. Include at least the primary attending physician.

period.title: Encounter Period
period.description: The start and end time of the encounter. For ongoing encounters, only 'start' is set.

reasonCode.title: Reason Codes
reasonCode.description: The coded reasons for the encounter — the chief complaint or presenting problem. Use SNOMED CT or ICD-10 codes.

diagnosis.title: Encounter Diagnoses
diagnosis.description: Diagnoses relevant to this encounter. Include at least the primary/chief complaint diagnosis with rank 1.

location.title: Encounter Locations
location.description: Physical locations where the encounter took place — rooms, wards, beds, etc.

priority.title: Encounter Priority
priority.description: The priority of the encounter — indicates urgency (e.g., 'routine', 'urgent', 'emergency').

EncounterCreateSchema.title: EncounterCreateSchema
EncounterCreateSchema.description:
Schema for creating a FHIR Encounter resource.

An Encounter is an interaction between a patient and healthcare provider(s) for the
purpose of providing healthcare services or assessing the health status of a patient.
This includes clinic visits, hospital admissions, emergency department visits, and
telehealth sessions. All fields follow the HL7 FHIR R4 Encounter specification.

Required fields: 'status' and 'class' (encounter classification).

condition.title: Condition Reference
condition.description: A FHIR reference to the Condition resource describing the diagnosis, in the format 'Condition/ID'.

use.title: Diagnosis Role
use.description: The role this diagnosis plays in the encounter — e.g. 'admission', 'discharge', 'billing', 'chief complaint'.

rank.title: Diagnosis Rank
rank.description: Ranking of the diagnosis for this encounter. 1 = primary diagnosis, 2 = secondary, etc.

EncounterDiagnosis.title: EncounterDiagnosis
EncounterDiagnosis.description: A diagnosis relevant to this encounter.

location.title: Location Reference
location.description: A FHIR reference to the Location resource, in the format 'Location/ID' or 'Location/room-3'.

status.title: Location Status
status.description: The status of the patient's presence at the location. 'planned' = expected, 'active' = currently there, 'completed' = has left.

period.title: Location Period
period.description: The time period during which the patient was at this location.

EncounterLocation.title: EncounterLocation
EncounterLocation.description: A physical location where the encounter takes place.

type.title: Participant Type
type.description: The role of this participant in the encounter, e.g. 'Primary Physician', 'Consulting Physician', 'Nurse'.

individual.title: Individual Reference
individual.description: A FHIR reference to the Practitioner or Patient who participated, in the format 'Practitioner/ID' or 'Patient/ID'.

period.title: Participation Period
period.description: The time period during which this individual participated in the encounter.

EncounterParticipant.title: EncounterParticipant
EncounterParticipant.description: A healthcare provider or person who participated in the encounter.

resourceType.title: Resource Type
resourceType.description: The FHIR resource type. Must always be 'Patient' for this schema.

active.title: Active Status
active.description: Whether this patient record is currently active and in use. Set to false to mark the record as inactive without deleting it.

identifier.title: Patient Identifiers
identifier.description: Business identifiers assigned to this patient — such as Medical Record Numbers (MRN), Social Security Numbers, or insurance IDs. At least one official identifier is recommended.

name.title: Patient Names
name.description: The names associated with the patient. A patient may have multiple names (e.g., legal name and nickname). Include at least one 'official' name.

telecom.title: Contact Points
telecom.description: Contact details for the patient — phone numbers, email addresses, etc. Order by preference rank when multiple contacts exist.

gender.title: Administrative Gender
gender.description: The administrative gender of the patient for record-keeping purposes. This is not the same as biological sex or gender identity.

birthDate.title: Date of Birth
birthDate.description: The date of birth of the patient in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

address.title: Patient Addresses
address.description: Physical or mailing addresses for the patient. Include at least the primary home address when available.

PatientCreateSchema.title: PatientCreateSchema
PatientCreateSchema.description:
Schema for creating a FHIR Patient resource.

A Patient resource covers data about individuals receiving care or other health-related
services. This includes demographics, contact details, and administrative information.
All fields follow the HL7 FHIR R4 Patient resource specification.

start.title: Start DateTime
start.description: The start of the period in ISO 8601 datetime format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). Inclusive boundary.

end.title: End DateTime
end.description: The end of the period in ISO 8601 datetime format. If absent, the period is ongoing.

Period.title: Period
Period.description: A time period defined by a start and/or end datetime.

resourceType.title: Resource Type
resourceType.description: The FHIR resource type. Must always be 'Practitioner' for this schema.

active.title: Active Status
active.description: Whether this practitioner record is currently active. Set to false if the practitioner is no longer practicing or has left the organization.

identifier.title: Practitioner Identifiers
identifier.description: Business identifiers for the practitioner — NPI numbers, medical license numbers, employee IDs, etc.

name.title: Practitioner Names
name.description: The names associated with the practitioner. Include professional prefixes like 'Dr.' and suffixes like 'MD'.

telecom.title: Contact Points
telecom.description: Contact details for the practitioner — primarily work phone, email, and pager numbers.

gender.title: Administrative Gender
gender.description: The administrative gender of the practitioner for record-keeping purposes.

birthDate.title: Date of Birth
birthDate.description: The practitioner's date of birth in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

address.title: Practitioner Addresses
address.description: Addresses associated with the practitioner, typically their practice or hospital location.

qualification.title: Professional Qualifications
qualification.description: Professional qualifications, certifications, accreditations, and licenses held by the practitioner.

PractitionerCreateSchema.title: PractitionerCreateSchema
PractitionerCreateSchema.description:
Schema for creating a FHIR Practitioner resource.

A Practitioner is a person who is directly or indirectly involved in the provisioning
of healthcare. This includes physicians, nurses, pharmacists, therapists, and other
clinical or administrative staff. All fields follow the HL7 FHIR R4 Practitioner specification.

identifier.title: Qualification Identifiers
identifier.description: Official identifiers for this qualification — license numbers, certification IDs, etc.

code.title: Qualification Code
code.description: The type of qualification — what degree, certification, or license this represents.

issuer.title: Issuing Organization
issuer.description: The organization that issued or certified the qualification.

Qualification.title: Qualification
Qualification.description: A professional qualification, certification, or accreditation held by the practitioner.

text.title: Qualification Description
text.description: A human-readable description of the qualification, e.g. 'MD - Doctor of Medicine', 'Board Certified in Cardiology'.

QualificationCode.title: QualificationCode
QualificationCode.description: A coded representation of the type of qualification.

system.title: Qualification System
system.description: The namespace URI for the qualification identifier, e.g. the medical board's system URL.

value.title: Qualification Number
value.description: The actual qualification or license number.

QualificationIdentifier.title: QualificationIdentifier
QualificationIdentifier.description: An identifier for a professional qualification or certification.

display.title: Issuer Name
display.description: The display name of the organization that issued the qualification, e.g. 'American Board of Medical Specialties'.

QualificationIssuer.title: QualificationIssuer
QualificationIssuer.description: The organization that issued or certifies the qualification.

reference.title: Resource Reference
reference.description: A relative reference to another FHIR resource in the format 'ResourceType/ID', e.g. 'Patient/123' or 'Practitioner/456'.

display.title: Display Text
display.description: A human-readable description of the referenced resource for display purposes.

Reference.title: Reference
Reference.description: A FHIR reference to another resource, using the format 'ResourceType/ID'.

use.title: Address Use
use.description: The purpose of this address. 'home' for the patient's residence, 'work' for employer address, 'billing' for billing purposes.

type.title: Address Type
type.description: Distinguishes between physical addresses (those you can visit) and mailing addresses (PO Boxes). Use 'both' if the address serves both purposes.

line.title: Street Address Lines
line.description: Street name, number, apartment/suite, PO Box, etc. Each element represents one line of the address.

city.title: City
city.description: The name of the city, town, or municipality.

state.title: State/Province
state.description: The state, province, or region abbreviation or name.

postalCode.title: Postal Code
postalCode.description: The postal or ZIP code for the address.

country.title: Country
country.description: The country name or ISO 3166 country code.

app__schemas__patient__Address.title: Address
app__schemas__patient__Address.description: A physical or postal address following FHIR Address datatype.

use.title: Name Use
use.description: Designates which name to use in a particular context. 'official' is the legal name. 'usual' is the name commonly used. 'nickname' for informal names. 'maiden' for pre-marriage family name.

family.title: Family Name
family.description: The family name (surname/last name) of the person.

given.title: Given Names
given.description: The given names (first name and middle names) of the person. First element is the primary given name.

text.title: Full Name Text
text.description: The complete human-readable name as a single string, typically formatted as 'Given Family'.

app__schemas__patient__HumanName.title: HumanName
app__schemas__patient__HumanName.description: A human name with structured components following FHIR HumanName datatype.

system.title: Identifier System
system.description: The namespace URI for the identifier value. Typically a URL that uniquely identifies the assigning authority, e.g. 'http://hospital.goodhealth.com/mrn'.

value.title: Identifier Value
value.description: The actual identifier string that uniquely identifies the resource within its system namespace, e.g. a Medical Record Number.

use.title: Identifier Use
use.description: The purpose of this identifier. 'official' means this is the primary identifier used by the organization. 'usual' is the most commonly used. 'temp' for temporary identifiers.

app__schemas__patient__Identifier.title: Identifier
app__schemas__patient__Identifier.description: A unique identifier assigned to a resource, such as a Medical Record Number (MRN).

system.title: Contact System
system.description: The type of communication system. Use 'phone' for landline or mobile numbers, 'email' for email addresses, 'sms' for SMS-capable numbers.

value.title: Contact Value
value.description: The actual contact point value — a phone number, email address, or URL depending on the system.

use.title: Contact Use
use.description: The purpose of this contact point. 'home' for personal use, 'work' for professional, 'mobile' specifically for mobile phones.

rank.title: Preference Rank
rank.description: Specifies a preferred order for using this contact. Lower numbers indicate higher preference (1 = most preferred).

app__schemas__patient__Telecom.title: Telecom
app__schemas__patient__Telecom.description: A contact point for a person — phone number, email, or other communication channel.

use.title: Address Use
use.description: The purpose of this address. 'work' for the practitioner's practice or hospital address.

type.title: Address Type
type.description: Distinguishes between a physical location and a mailing address.

line.title: Street Address Lines
line.description: Street name, number, suite, building, etc.

city.title: City
city.description: The name of the city, town, or municipality.

country.title: Country
country.description: The country name or ISO 3166 country code.

app__schemas__practitioner__Address.title: Address
app__schemas__practitioner__Address.description: A physical or mailing address for the practitioner, typically their practice location.

use.title: Name Use
use.description: Designates which name to use in a particular context. 'official' is the legal name, 'usual' is the commonly used professional name.

family.title: Family Name
family.description: The family name (surname/last name) of the practitioner.

given.title: Given Names
given.description: The given names (first name and middle names) of the practitioner.

text.title: Full Name Text
text.description: The complete human-readable name as a single string, including any professional titles.

prefix.title: Name Prefixes
prefix.description: Professional or honorific prefixes such as 'Dr.', 'Prof.', 'Mr.', 'Mrs.'.

suffix.title: Name Suffixes
suffix.description: Professional or generational suffixes such as 'Jr.', 'Sr.', 'PhD', 'MD'.

app__schemas__practitioner__HumanName.title: HumanName
app__schemas__practitioner__HumanName.description: A practitioner's name including professional prefixes and suffixes.

system.title: Identifier System
system.description: The namespace URI for the identifier value. Typically a URL identifying the assigning authority, e.g. 'http://hospital.goodhealth.com/practitioners'.

value.title: Identifier Value
value.description: The actual identifier that uniquely identifies this practitioner within the namespace — e.g., a medical license number or employee ID.

use.title: Identifier Use
use.description: The purpose of this identifier. 'official' for the primary organizational identifier, 'usual' for commonly used identifiers.

app__schemas__practitioner__Identifier.title: Identifier
app__schemas__practitioner__Identifier.description: A unique identifier assigned to a practitioner, such as a medical license number or NPI.

system.title: Contact System
system.description: The type of communication system. Use 'phone' for phone numbers, 'email' for email addresses, 'pager' for hospital paging systems.

value.title: Contact Value
value.description: The actual contact point value — a phone number, email address, or URL.

use.title: Contact Use
use.description: The purpose of this contact point. Most practitioner contacts are 'work'. Use 'mobile' for personal mobile numbers.

rank.title: Preference Rank
rank.description: Preferred order for using this contact. Lower numbers = higher preference (1 = most preferred).

app__schemas__practitioner__Telecom.title: Telecom
app__schemas__practitioner__Telecom.description: A contact point for the practitioner — phone, email, or other communication channel.

tags.name: Patient
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Patient resources — individuals receiving care. Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.

tags.name: Practitioner
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Practitioner resources — healthcare providers such as physicians, nurses, and therapists. Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.

tags.name: Encounter
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Encounter resources — clinical interactions between patients and providers (visits, admissions, telehealth). Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.
